title: Safe water for the immunocompromised (household practice)
context: People who are immunocompromised (e.g., due to HIV/AIDS, chemotherapy, transplant) have heightened vulnerability to waterborne pathogens. Guidance on water safety for immunocompromised individuals requires clinical sign-off because the thresholds and recommendations may differ from general population guidance. This is a HIGH-risk backlog unit, gated on a secured licensed clinician (partner-014). Scope is household practice and 'when-to-consult-a-clinician'; not clinical treatment.
objective: Author, source-cite, clinician-sign-off, and independently numeric-verify a WashUnit covering safe water practices for immunocompromised individuals at the household level — focusing on heightened precautions, appropriate water treatment methods, and when to consult a clinician — with required disclaimer and danger-signs/when-to-seek-care content.
acceptanceCriteria: Every claim tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/CDC/relevant clinical guidelines) with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Every numeric value is independently second-checked and confirmed by the secured licensed clinician (expertConfirmed:true on all numericClaims); a recorded expertSignOff is attached before merge

Unit includes explicit 'when-to-consult-a-clinician' content; carries the required 'informational, not a substitute for professional medical care' disclaimer; scope is household practice only (no clinical treatment instruction)
CI HIGH-risk gate (ci-018) passes: expertSignOff present, all numericClaims verified and expertConfirmed:true, disclaimer field present
Reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author and the expert; PR-tied review-log entry written
output: A committed, clinician-signed-off, independently numeric-verified WashUnit covering safe water practices for immunocompromised individuals at the household level, with disclaimer, when-to-consult content, and PR-tied review-log entry.
